name: Tunnel Zone 2 — The Karst Road Wandering Monsters
description: Wandering monster table for Zone 2 (Days 6–10): Wider tunnels, bioluminescent fungi, evidence of regular giant traffic. More organized creatures moving in. Check frequency: once per 8 hours of travel, once per 8 hours of rest.

results.text: 1d6+2 Ghouls (drawn to the giant campsites) [PF2e Lvl 2 each]
1 Ogre (lone scout, carrying a message for the Steading) [PF2e Lvl 3]
2d4 Troglodytes with 1 Troglodyte King [PF2e Lvl 2 / Lvl 5]
1 Wyvern (nested in a high chamber, hunting) [PF2e Lvl 6]
1 Cave Bear (territorial, not aggressive if given space) [PF2e Lvl 4]
2d6 Giant Bats (disturbed by torchlight) [PF2e Lvl 2 each]
1 Basilisk (petrified prey scattered around it) [PF2e Lvl 5]
1 Doppelganger (impersonating a recently dead traveler) [PF2e Lvl 3]
1 Will-o'-Wisp (leads party toward Zone 1 hazards) [PF2e Lvl 6]
1 Troll + 1d4 Ogres (troll leads a raiding pack) [PF2e Lvl 5 / Lvl 3]
1d6 Grimlocks armed with giant-made weapons [PF2e Lvl 2 each]
1 Animated Giant Skeleton (remains of a hill giant, risen) [PF2e Lvl 7]
3 Ogres (returning to Steading, drunk on fermented something) [PF2e Lvl 3 each]
1 Stone Giant (lost; neutral; knows the Steading exists) [PF2e Lvl 9]
HAZARD — Fungal Bloom: spores fill a 60-foot section; DC 16 Fortitude or stupefied 1 for 24 hours
